name: libhttp-dav-perl
title: libhttp-dav-perl
summary: WebDAV client library for Perl, and "dave" CLI client
description:
WebDAV client library for Perl, and "dave" CLI client
HTTP::DAV provides client-side access to a DAV server.  DAV is a
protocol for remote web-site authoring and management.  Now you can
LOCK, DELETE and PUT files and much more on a DAV-enabled webserver.
.
The PerlDAV library consists of:
.
* HTTP::DAV - an object-oriented Web-DAV client API.
* dave - the DAV Explorer, an end-user Unix console program for
interacting with WebDAV servers. dave looks and feels like a
standard Unix ftp program.
.
Learn more about WebDAV at http://www.webdav.org/

arguments.description: Sets the username to be used for the URL. You must also supply a pass. See "-p".
arguments.raw: -u  Sets the username to be used for the URL. You must also supply a pass. See "-p".

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dave because WebDAV client library for Perl, and "dave" CLI client.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dave as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dave [OPTIONS] URL` as documented upstream for dave.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dave> get file.txt` as documented upstream for dave.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dave with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dave --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install libhttp-dav-perl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dave> get file.txt`. WebDAV client library for Perl, and "dave" CLI client.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: WebDAV client library for Perl, and "dave" CLI client.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: WebDAV client library for Perl, and "dave" CLI client.
